body_txt: At its simplest form, a character reveals himself to be an undercover police officer, FBI agent, what have you. Often, he's acting as The Mole , trying to get enough evidence on the villain to arrest him for Arson, Murder, and Jaywalking . It's entirely possible that we, the viewers, won't find out until the rest of the characters do as well. Depending on what side of the Sliding Scale of Idealism Versus Cynicism , this may or may not end badly for the cop. Subtrope of The Reveal . Closely related to The Infiltration , which involves a law enforcement officer pretending to be a criminal in order to join/infiltrate the bad guys' organization. Compare Booked Full of Mooks . Contrast Can Always Spot a Cop , where police officers are too obvious to ever get very far in their efforts to go undercover. Compare and contrast Must State If You're a Cop where a police officer is forced to do this by someone else. Impersonating an Officer is basically the inverse of this trope.
